title: Smart Compost Monitor
description: Wireless compost bin monitoring system tracking temperature at three depths, surface moisture, and headspace CO₂ concentration. An ESP32 reads sensors and publishes readings to an MQTT broker over WiFi; an OLED shows live data locally. Alerts when temperature drops out of the optimal thermophilic range or moisture falls below threshold.
objective: Monitor compost temperature at 3 depths (0–70 °C), moisture (0–100 %), and CO₂ (0–5000 ppm); MQTT publish every 2 min; alert if T < 40 °C during active phase or moisture < 40 %; battery-backed in case of power outage.
constraints: Mains USB power (5 V 2 A); outdoor IP54 sensor probe enclosure; WiFi on home LAN; MQTT broker (Mosquitto); ≤ £30 BOM.
supporting_fact_titles: Aerobic Composting — Temperature, Moisture, and Gas Indicators
MQTT Protocol for IoT Sensor Data

element_usage_notes.DS18B20 1-Wire Temperature Probe Array: Three waterproof DS18B20 probes on one 1-Wire bus (D4); 12-bit mode; probes at 15 cm / 30 cm / 45 cm depth via 50 cm stainless cables; 4.7 kΩ pull-up.
element_usage_notes.Capacitive Soil Moisture Probe: One v2.0 capacitive probe adapted for compost (silicone-sealed); analog output on A0; calibrated at air=dry (0 %) and saturated peat (100 %).
element_usage_notes.SCD41 NDIR CO₂ Sensing Module: I²C address 0x62; periodic measurement every 5 s; headspace probe tube inserted 5 cm into compost mound; reports CO₂ ppm + T + RH.
element_usage_notes.SSD1306 OLED Display UI Module: 128×64; 4-line layout: T1/T2/T3 / moisture % / CO₂ ppm / status (ACTIVE/COOLING/DONE).
element_usage_notes.ESP32 WiFi + MQTT IoT Telemetry: Broker at 192.168.1.x; topic compost/{sensor}; QoS 1; publishes every 120 s; retained messages for HA dashboard.
element_usage_notes.MQTT QoS-1 Secure Event Logger: Alert events (T out of range, dry, CO₂ spike) published to compost/alerts with timestamp.

element_interactions.description: Dallas Temperature library enumerates three ROM addresses; reads all three after requestTemperatures(); values averaged over 3 readings before MQTT publish.

element_interactions.data: moisture_pct (float) from ADC1 channel on GPIO 36
element_interactions.description: ESP32 ADC1 (GPIO 36) samples 32× averaged; linear map from calibrated dry_raw and wet_raw to 0–100 %. Alert fired if < 40 % and T > 40 °C (active phase).
SCD41 read every 5 s; 3-sample median used for MQTT payload. CO₂ > 3000 ppm triggers 'CO2_HIGH' alert (indicates very active aerobic decomposition).

element_interactions.data: I²C framebuffer update (temp array, moisture, co2, status)
element_interactions.description: OLED updated at 2 Hz locally regardless of MQTT publish interval. Status line: ACTIVE if T > 45 °C; COOLING if 35–45 °C; DONE if < 35 °C + CO₂ < 1000 ppm.

element_interactions.data: JSON alert payload {alert_type, value, threshold, ts}
element_interactions.description: Alert conditions: T[0] < 40 °C for 30 min during active phase; moisture < 40 %; CO₂ > 3000 ppm. Each distinct alert published once per hour to avoid flooding.
